sections.title: The Price of Intelligence is Collapsing

paragraphs.source_preview: Now software engineering has and always will be the gold standard information work. But as the quality has finally crossed over a critical threshold, the relati
The cost of Claude Pro or ChatGPT is $20 dollars a month, while a Max subscription is $200 dollars respectively. The median US knowledge worker costs ~350-500 d
Enterprise is Already Starting to Move
The massive deflationary cost of intelligence is going to reprice every information company’s margin for repeatable work. Accenture just signed a deal to train
Enterprise software has easily been the first casualty of the great cost decline of intelligence. SaaS itself is just crystalized information processing of work
A simplistic example is an agent can now query a Postgres database directly on your behalf, generate a chart, and email it to a stakeholder. That was effectivel
In our view, anything that has a human click buttons, gather information, reformat it into another medium (email, chart, excel, presentation) is a huge risk. LL
The cost collapse is destroying the seat-based software model. And with the massive adoption of Claude Code internally at SemiAnalysis, there has been no bigger
Why does a company need to standardize Salesforce if an agent is just going to query data on leads on your behalf? Salesforce is a form and workflow wrapper, an
We recently (wrongly) called for an acceleration in Microsoft’s revenue, mostly driven by their large rental fleet and a shift to external foundry capacity. But
And much of the acceleration that I think you’ve seen from us and products over the past a bit is coming because we are allocating GPUs and capacity to many of
The important bit of context is this:
We’re really making long-term decisions. And the first thing we’re doing is solving for the increased usage in sales and the accelerating pace of M365 Copilot a
There are two beasts within Microsoft: Azure growth for public market investors and investing in Copilot to preserve the Office 365 product suite. To decisively
Claude for Excel effectively is what Copilot for Excel should have been, but it was launched by an external party on their own first party product. Most of the
Microsoft’s spending on AI ironically must increase or the terminal value of the O365 suite of products is going to plummet. They do have distribution, but it’s
GitHub copilot and Office Copilot had a year headstart and barely made any inroads as a product. Meanwhile Satya is literally stepping in as the product manager

paragraphs.source_preview: One of the most interesting questions in this debate: GPT-5.2 High has been benchmark-maxxing for a year and held pole position for the past two years since Cha

Long-horizon performance is fundamentally about compounding signal over multiple steps across context windows. Even though ChatGPT has a much larger context win

If you need 90% performance on a single context window, compounded over X steps, even small error rates guarantee failure at the end.
